theorem_statement:
Finite-time blow-up from a virial identity for focusing mass-critical NLS.

Let \(u(t,x)\) be a sufficiently regular solution on \(\mathbb{R}^d\) with finite variance. Assume:

1. mass is conserved:

\[
\|u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}=\|u_0\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}=:M>0,
\]

2. energy is conserved and negative:

\[
E(u(t))=E(u_0)<0,
\]

3. the variance

\[
V(t)=\int_{\mathbb{R}^d}|x|^2|u(t,x)|^2\,dx
\]

satisfies

\[
V''(t)=16E(u_0)<0,
\]

4. for every sufficiently regular \(f\),

\[
\|f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}^2
\le
\frac{2}{d}\,\|x f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\,\|\nabla f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}.
\]

Then there exists \(T<\infty\) such that

\[
\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\to\infty
\quad\text{as } t\uparrow T.
\]

nodes.title: Finite-time blow-up from virial collapse of the variance
nodes.informal_statement:
There exists \(T<\infty\) such that
\[
\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\to\infty
\quad\text{as } t\uparrow T.
\]
nodes.informal_proof_text:
Use the estimate
\[
M^2\le \frac{2}{d}\sqrt{V(t)}\,\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}
\]
from mass conservation and the weighted inequality, together with the existence of a finite time \(T>0\) such that \(V(t)\to 0\) as \(t\uparrow T\). Since \(M>0\) is fixed, the right-hand side can stay bounded below by \(M^2\) only if
\[
\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\to\infty
\quad\text{as } t\uparrow T.
\]
Thus the solution blows up in finite positive time.

nodes.title: Mass/variance-gradient estimate
nodes.informal_statement:
For every time in the interval of existence,
\[
M^2=\|u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}^2
\le
\frac{2}{d}\sqrt{V(t)}\,\|\nabla u(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}.
\]
nodes.informal_proof_text:
Apply the assumed weighted inequality
\[
\|f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}^2\le \frac{2}{d}\,\|xf\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}\,\|\nabla f\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}
\]
with \(f=u(t)\). Mass conservation gives \(\|u(t)\|_{L^2}=M\), and by definition of the variance,
\[
\|xu(t)\|_{L^2(\mathbb{R}^d)}^2=V(t),
\]
so \(\|xu(t)\|_{L^2}=\sqrt{V(t)}\). Substituting yields the claimed estimate.

nodes.title: Negative virial curvature forces finite-time collapse of the variance
nodes.informal_statement:
There exists a finite time \(T>0\) such that
\[
V(T)=0,
\]
and hence
\[
V(t)\to 0\quad\text{as }t\uparrow T.
\]
nodes.informal_proof_text:
From the virial identity and \(E(u_0)<0\),
\[
V''(t)=16E(u_0)<0
\]
for all times of existence. Integrating twice gives
\[
V(t)=8E(u_0)t^2+at+b
\]
for some constants \(a,b\), so the coefficient of \(t^2\) is negative. Therefore \(V(t)\to -\infty\) as \(t\to +\infty\). On the other hand,
\[
V(t)=\int_{\mathbb{R}^d}|x|^2|u(t,x)|^2\,dx\ge 0
\]
whenever the solution exists. Hence \(V\) cannot remain positive for all \(t\ge 0\). By continuity, there is a finite \(T>0\) with \(V(T)=0\), equivalently \(V(t)\to 0\) as \(t\uparrow T\).

nodes.title: Local estimate
nodes.informal_proof_text:
Apply the assumed weighted inequality
\[

\]
with \(f=u(t)\). Mass conservation gives \(\|u(t)\|_{L^2}=M\), and by definition of the variance,

nodes.formalization_rationale: Smaller concrete local consequence extracted because it is more directly formalizable than the broader surrounding node.

edges.label: depends on
edges.explanation: Parent proof uses a more concrete downstream consequence extracted from its local argument.

edges.label: uses
edges.explanation: The blow-up conclusion uses that the variance tends to zero at some finite positive time.
